There is abundance.<|27.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The kingdom of heaven is where followers of Jesus do the work of the kingdom.<|9.70|> <|9.70|> Can it be that the kingdom of heaven is here and now, and we are the workers?<|17.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.92|> Just as Richard shared in his sermon last week, the smallest of efforts, the smallest of things,<|8.94|> <|9.44|> even as small as a mustard seed, can produce abundance.<|14.16|> <|15.60|> As canon pastor at St. John's, I get to see this kingdom work enacted every day.<|23.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Even in the midst of a pandemic, our people's compassion continues to spur them to action.<|8.34|> <|9.34|> I see individuals who don't think they are doing much that is extraordinary,<|14.80|> <|16.14|> adding their efforts and care to a host of others who are doing the same.<|21.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> name.<|1.44|> <|1.44|> These efforts multiply and create an extravagant outpouring of care shared in our community.<|11.78|> <|11.78|> Our Eucharistic visitors continue to call and check on the folks they visited before<|17.32|> <|17.32|> the COVID-19 pandemic.<|18.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Our spiritual directors continue to meet virtually with their directees.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> The stargazers continue to reach out to the widows in our midst,<|12.00|> <|12.00|> especially those who have become widowed in the past several months.<|18.00|> <|18.00|> Our Daughters of the King chapter continues to meet virtually,<|22.00|> <|22.00|> train new members, and devote themselves to intercessory prayer for our<|28.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> our community. Cards are still sent on one year anniversaries of baptisms, weddings,<|8.96|> <|8.96|> and funerals. A number of our folks stepped up to adopt a parishioner who would be particularly<|17.40|> <|17.40|> vulnerable and isolated during this time of quarantine, pledging to call frequently and<|25.66|> <|25.66|> send notes of encouragement.<|27.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Over 40 parishioners recently undertook a calling campaign to check in on as many folks<|9.28|> <|9.28|> as possible and to date have completed almost 450 phone calls. Compassion turned to death.<|20.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> action. Followers of Jesus setting aside their own personal pain and loss. And my<|8.04|> <|8.04|> friends, we are all experiencing pain and loss right now. Setting that aside to be<|16.92|> <|16.92|> present to others. To bring a sense of wholeness and abundance to those who need<|24.50|> <|24.50|> it the most. To those who are<|26.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> alone. Compassion turned to action. The actions of individuals joining others<|9.18|> <|9.18|> until an entire community is transformed. The kingdom of heaven enacted here and now.<|19.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Your church is not closed.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> Your church is alive and well.<|9.00|> <|9.00|> Your church is doing kingdom work.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> Your church is doing the miracle work that grows from compassion,<|20.00|> <|20.00|> creating connection and reminding people of the abundance that is possible when we work together.<|28.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The kingdom of heaven is like this.<|4.82|> <|6.90|> Be well, my friends.<|8.58|> <|9.44|> I miss you.<|10.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
